articles.title: HBO Harry Potter Series Lands 2027 Date with Dominic McLaughlin as Harry
articles.summary: HBO's Harry Potter television series is officially in production and is set to arrive in 2027, with Dominic McLaughlin cast as Harry Potter himself. A first photo of McLaughlin in character has already been released, and Season 2 has already been confirmed before Season 1 even airs. The show is shaping up to be one of the biggest TV events in years.
articles.content:
After years of speculation, the HBO Harry Potter series is real, it is filming, and we now have a face to put to the Boy Who Lived. Dominic McLaughlin has been cast as Harry Potter, and HBO has already released a photo of him in the role, complete with the glasses and the scar. He looks the part.

For anyone who needs a quick recap: HBO announced it was adapting all seven Harry Potter books into a long-form television series, with the idea being that each book gets a full season of its episodes. That means far more detail than the original film series, which ran from 2001 to 2011 and was forced to cut huge chunks of J.K. Rowling's story to fit into two-hour movies. The TV format gives the writers room to breathe.

The big news this week is not just that production has started. It is that Season 2 has already been greenlit. HBO is committing to the long game here before a single episode has aired. That is a serious vote of confidence, and it tells you how much is riding on this show commercially. Harry Potter is still one of the most valuable entertainment properties on the planet.

J.K. Rowling is involved with the series, which has been a point of conversation given the controversy around her public statements on gender over the past several years. Her involvement has divided fans, with some excited to have the original author connected to the adaptation and others choosing to keep their distance. HBO has not shied away from acknowledging her role.

The casting of younger, unknown actors in the central roles follows the same logic as the original films did when they chose an eleven-year-old Daniel Radcliffe back in 2001. Fresh faces mean the audience meets Harry, Ron, and Hermione without the baggage of seeing a celebrity first. If the writing is strong, that choice could pay off in a big way.

With a 2027 target and Season 2 already confirmed, HBO is clearly building this as the next Game of Thrones-sized franchise for the network. Whether it lands that way depends on how well the first season handles the source material. But right now, the momentum is real.
keyPoints: HBO's Harry Potter TV series is in production and is targeting a 2027 release
Dominic McLaughlin has been cast as Harry Potter and a first photo has been released
Season 2 has already been confirmed before Season 1 has aired
The series will adapt each of the seven Harry Potter books as its own season
J.K. Rowling is involved with the production

articles.title: Bukayo Saka Sends Arsenal to First Champions League Final in 20 Years
articles.summary: Bukayo Saka scored the only goal as Arsenal beat Atletico Madrid 1-0 at the Emirates Stadium on Wednesday, reaching the Champions League final for the first time since 2006. Mikel Arteta's side will now go to the biggest club game in European football. Arsenal fans have been waiting two decades for this moment.
articles.content:
Twenty years is a long time to wait. Arsenal last appeared in the Champions League final back in 2006, losing to Barcelona in Paris. A generation of supporters has grown up never seeing their club on that stage. On Wednesday 6 May 2026, Bukayo Saka ended that wait with one goal at the Emirates Stadium.

The match against Atletico Madrid was tight, tense, and exactly the kind of game that goes either way. Arsenal came into it knowing a 1-0 win would be enough, and Saka delivered precisely that. The 24-year-old winger, who has become the heartbeat of this Arsenal side under manager Mikel Arteta, scored the only goal of the game to send the crowd into a frenzy and book Arsenal's place in the final.

For context, the UEFA Champions League is the biggest club football competition in Europe, featuring the top teams from leagues across the continent. Reaching the final is the holy grail for any club, and Arsenal have not been close to it in a very long time. Mikel Arteta, who has managed Arsenal since 2019, has rebuilt the club from a mid-table mess into genuine European contenders. This result is the biggest moment of his tenure so far.

Bukayo Saka has had a complicated relationship with high-pressure moments. He was one of three England players to miss a penalty in the Euro 2020 final shootout, an experience that haunted him publicly for months. The way he has bounced back, game after game, and made himself Arsenal's most important player is one of football's best recent stories. Wednesday night was another chapter in that comeback.

Arsenal now head to the Champions League final, and the football world will be watching.
keyPoints: Bukayo Saka scored the only goal as Arsenal beat Atletico Madrid 1-0
Arsenal are in the Champions League final for the first time since 2006
Manager Mikel Arteta has transformed Arsenal into genuine European contenders
Saka has become Arsenal's most important and clutch player under Arteta
The result ends a 20-year wait for Arsenal fans to see their club in the final

articles.title: Hayden Panettiere Comes Out as Bisexual at 36, Feared Her Team's Reaction
articles.summary: Hayden Panettiere has come out as bisexual at age 36, telling Variety she held back for years because she feared public opinion and worried about what her team was going to think. The Heroes and Nashville actress said the fear of professional and public judgment kept her from speaking her truth sooner. Her candid admission about the specific fear of her own management and PR team is a rare and honest look at how the entertainment industry quietly polices identity.
articles.content:
Hayden Panettiere is finally living out loud. The actress, best known for playing Claire Bennet in Heroes and Juliette Barnes in Nashville, has come out as bisexual at 36 years old in a new interview with Variety. And she did not sugarcoat why it took this long.

Panettiere said directly that she feared public opinion and, perhaps more strikingly, feared what her team was going to think. That second part hits differently. It is one thing to worry about how fans or the press will react. It is another to say that the very people paid to support your career were a source of anxiety around your own identity. That kind of honesty is rare in Hollywood.

Hayden Panettiere has had a very public life since she was a child actress. She has dealt with tabloid scrutiny around her relationships, her health, and her personal struggles including her well-documented battle with postpartum depression and addiction recovery. Coming out in the middle of all that history takes real courage, and she clearly wanted to do it on her own terms.

The entertainment industry has made progress on LGBTQ+ visibility, but Panettiere's admission shows the behind-the-scenes pressure has not disappeared. Publicists, managers, and agents still shape how stars present themselves, and that influence does not always leave room for authenticity. Her speaking about it so plainly is genuinely useful for the conversation.

Panettiere is 36 now and clearly at a point in her life where she is done letting fear make decisions for her. She has rebuilt publicly after some very hard years, and this feels like another step in owning her story completely. For anyone who has ever felt pressured to hide who they are by the people around them, her words are going to land hard.
keyPoints: Hayden Panettiere has come out as bisexual at age 36 in a Variety interview
She says she delayed coming out due to fear of public opinion and her own team's reaction
Panettiere is known for Heroes and Nashville
Her honesty about industry pressure on identity is unusually candid for a public figure
The admission comes after years of personal and public challenges she has spoken openly about

articles.title: BTS Greets 50,000 Fans from Mexico's Presidential Palace
articles.summary: BTS addressed a massive crowd of 50,000 ARMY fans from the balcony of Mexico City's National Palace on Thursday after meeting with President Claudia Sheinbaum. The moment was a cultural milestone, blending K-pop diplomacy with one of Latin America's most iconic political stages. BTS told the crowd, 'The energy here is incredible.'
articles.content:
BTS made history on Thursday in a way that no K-pop act has before. After sitting down with Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum at the National Palace in Mexico City, the seven-member group stepped out onto the balcony and spoke directly to 50,000 ARMY fans who had packed the Zócalo, the massive public square at the heart of the city, and waited there for hours just for this moment.

The Zócalo is one of the largest public squares in the world and sits right in front of the National Palace, which is the official seat of Mexico's federal government. Having a K-pop group address a crowd from that balcony is genuinely unprecedented. Mexican presidents use that space for national celebrations. BTS being there signals just how seriously the Mexican government is taking the group's cultural influence.

BTS told the crowd, 'The energy here is incredible,' and by all accounts the crowd agreed. Videos flooding social media showed tens of thousands of fans screaming, waving light sticks, and singing along. The scene looked more like a stadium concert than a diplomatic visit, which is kind of the whole point. BTS has always existed at the intersection of music and something larger.

This visit comes as BTS members have been returning from mandatory South Korean military service, with the full group now reuniting for public appearances and scheduled activity in 2026. Mexico has consistently been one of BTS's biggest markets outside Asia, and this visit cements that relationship in the most dramatic way possible. Fifty thousand people standing in a plaza waiting for a glimpse from a palace balcony is the kind of devotion that no marketing budget can manufacture.
keyPoints: BTS met with Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum at the National Palace
50,000 ARMY fans packed the Zócalo in Mexico City and waited hours for the moment
BTS addressed the crowd from the palace balcony, saying 'The energy here is incredible'
The appearance is historic as no K-pop act has addressed a crowd from this political venue before
BTS members have been reuniting in 2026 after completing South Korean military service

articles.title: CNN Founder Ted Turner Dies at 87
articles.summary: Ted Turner, the media mogul who launched CNN in 1980 and changed television forever, died on Wednesday at the age of 87. Turner built one of the most influential media empires in history, creating the world's first 24-hour news network at a time when most people thought the idea was crazy. His ex-wife Jane Fonda remembered their marriage with warmth, saying she was grateful for the years they shared.
articles.content:
Ted Turner is gone at 87, and it is genuinely hard to overstate what he meant to television and to the world. He founded CNN in 1980, which was the first 24-hour cable news network ever. People in the industry laughed at him. They called it "Chicken Noodle News." He proved every single one of them wrong.

Before CNN, you got the news at 6pm and again at 11pm, and that was it. Turner changed that completely. Suddenly, news was on whenever you wanted it. When major events happened, from wars to elections to disasters, CNN was there live. It redefined what people expected from journalism and from television itself.

Turner was not just a TV guy. He was a sailor who won the America's Cup in 1977, a baseball team owner who brought the Atlanta Braves back from the brink, and one of the most generous philanthropists in American history. He donated a billion dollars to the United Nations in 1997, which at the time was one of the largest charitable gifts ever given by a private individual.

His personal life was just as big as his public one. He was married to actress and activist Jane Fonda from 1991 to 2001. Fonda responded to news of his death by remembering their marriage with clear affection, saying she was grateful for the time they had together. Ted Turner was one of those rare people who actually reshaped the world. The 24-hour news cycle, for better or worse, is his legacy.
keyPoints: Ted Turner died on Wednesday 6 May 2026 at the age of 87
Turner founded CNN in 1980, the world's first 24-hour cable news network
He was also a champion sailor, Atlanta Braves owner, and major philanthropist
In 1997 he donated one billion dollars to the United Nations
Ex-wife Jane Fonda remembered their marriage with affection after his death
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Ted Turner media mogul CNN founder

articles.title: No Doubt Opens Sphere Residency: Every Song They Played
articles.summary: No Doubt, the ska-punk band led by Gwen Stefani alongside Tony Kanal, Tom Dumont, and Adrian Young, opened their Las Vegas Sphere residency on Wednesday night, kicking off 12 scheduled shows at the venue. The setlist gave fans their first clear look at what the full residency will deliver. Sphere's wraparound immersive screen technology makes it one of the most visually intense concert venues ever built.
articles.content:
No Doubt officially brought their Las Vegas residency to life on Wednesday night at Sphere, the giant spherical venue on the Las Vegas Strip that has become the most talked-about concert space in the world since it opened in 2023. The show was the first of 12 dates the quartet have booked there, and it gave fans a proper preview of what the full run is going to look like.

The band, which is Gwen Stefani, Tony Kanal, Tom Dumont, and Adrian Young, is one of the most beloved acts of the 1990s and early 2000s. Their albums Tragic Kingdom and Return of Saturn turned them into global stars, and songs like 'Don't Speak', 'Just a Girl', and 'Spiderwebs' still hit hard decades later. No Doubt went on an extended hiatus after 2015, making this residency a genuine reunion event for fans who have waited years to see them live again.

Sphere is not a normal venue. The inside is covered in a wraparound LED screen that stretches across the entire interior, which means the visuals are not just a backdrop but a full sensory environment surrounding the audience. Acts like U2 and Eagles have used it to create shows that felt more like experiences than concerts. No Doubt stepping into that space with their colourful, high-energy aesthetic is a strong match.

The full setlist from opening night is now public, giving fans who have tickets to later shows a sense of what is coming, and giving everyone else a reason to seriously consider grabbing a seat before the 12-show run wraps up. No Doubt at Sphere is the rare combination of nostalgia and spectacle that does not come around often.
keyPoints: No Doubt opened their Sphere residency in Las Vegas on Wednesday night
The residency runs for 12 total shows at Sphere
The band is Gwen Stefani, Tony Kanal, Tom Dumont, and Adrian Young
No Doubt had been on an extended hiatus since 2015 before this reunion
Sphere's full wraparound LED screen makes it one of the most immersive concert venues in the world

articles.title: Khloé Kardashian Says She Was Drugged at Coachella
articles.summary: Khloé Kardashian has claimed she was drugged during her time at Coachella 2026, making the allegation publicly via Page Six. The claim adds to a growing conversation about safety at major music festivals. Kardashian has not yet named a suspect or disclosed further details about the incident.
articles.content:
Khloé Kardashian is speaking out about a deeply unsettling experience. The reality TV star and Kardashians cast member says she was drugged while attending Coachella, the massive annual music and arts festival held in Indio, California. She made the claim public on May 7, 2026, and it immediately sent shockwaves through social media.

Coachella draws hundreds of thousands of people every year and has long been a fixture of celebrity culture. But it has also faced ongoing scrutiny over crowd safety and substance-related incidents. Kardashian's allegation puts a very public spotlight on that issue once again.

At this stage, Khloé Kardashian has not publicly named anyone she holds responsible, and no formal charges have been reported. What she has made clear is that something happened to her at the festival that she did not consent to. That alone is a serious allegation and one that deserves to be taken seriously.

Kardashian has a massive platform and a history of being open about difficult personal moments, from her health scares to her relationships. Her willingness to speak out here could encourage others who have had similar experiences at festivals to come forward. This story is still developing, and more details are expected to emerge.
keyPoints: Khloé Kardashian claims she was drugged at Coachella 2026
She made the allegation public on May 7, 2026 via Page Six
No suspect has been publicly named and no charges have been reported
The claim puts renewed attention on safety issues at major music festivals
The story is still developing with more details expected

articles.title: Ken Griffin Says NYC Mayor Mamdani Put Him in Harm's Way
articles.summary: Billionaire hedge fund founder Ken Griffin is firing back at New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani after Mamdani posted a viral video on Tax Day targeting Griffin's $239 million Manhattan penthouse. Griffin says Mamdani's video put him in physical danger by broadcasting his home address to millions of people. The clash has turned into one of the most high-profile wealth-versus-politics moments of 2026.
articles.content:
New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani made a campaign promise to tax the rich, and on Tax Day last month he made good on it in the most social-media-friendly way possible. He posted a viral video calling out Ken Griffin's $239 million Manhattan penthouse, saying directly to the camera: "When I ran for mayor, I said I was going to tax the rich. Today, we're taxing the rich." The video spread everywhere fast.

Ken Griffin is the founder of Citadel, one of the most powerful hedge funds in the world. He is worth tens of billions of dollars and is one of the wealthiest people in America. He is also famously private about his personal security. So when Mamdani's video drew a giant public arrow pointing at his home, Griffin did not stay quiet.

Griffin's response was sharp and personal. He said Mamdani "put me in harm's way" by broadcasting the location of his residence to a massive online audience. That is not a small claim. Wealthy public figures who have their home addresses spread online face real security risks, and Griffin is arguing that Mamdani's stunt crossed a serious line in the name of political theatre.

Mamdani's move fits a broader pattern in New York City politics right now. He ran as an unapologetically left-wing candidate and won, and he is now governing that way. Targeting a $239 million penthouse on Tax Day is the kind of move that plays very well with his base and very badly with the city's billionaire class. Griffin, notably, has previously spent significant money on Florida, where he relocated Citadel's headquarters to Miami in 2022, partly citing the political climate in blue cities.

This fight is about more than one video and one penthouse. It is a live public argument about who New York City is for, who gets to live there, and what elected officials are allowed to say. Griffin has the money and the platform to push back hard. Mamdani has the mayor's office and the viral moment. Neither is backing down, and the rest of us get to watch it play out in real time.
keyPoints: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani posted a viral Tax Day video calling out Ken Griffin's $239 million penthouse
Griffin responded by saying Mamdani put him in physical harm's way by publicising his home location
Griffin is the founder of Citadel, one of the world's biggest hedge funds
Griffin previously moved Citadel's headquarters from Chicago to Miami in 2022
The clash reflects a wider political battle over wealth and housing in New York City
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Ken Griffin Citadel hedge fund billionaire

articles.title: Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton Hold Hands in Public
articles.summary: Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton were spotted holding hands for the first time in public on Wednesday night in New York City, making their rumoured relationship impossible to ignore. The two were leaving the Broadway play The Fear of 13, which stars Adrien Brody. Kardashian wore a corset outfit for the evening out, and the hand-holding moment was caught on camera.
articles.content:
Well, it is officially official. Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton stepped out together in New York City on Wednesday night, and this time they were holding hands in public for the first time. That is the kind of detail that turns a rumour into a reality.

The two were leaving The Fear of 13, a Broadway play starring Adrien Brody. It is a theatrical thriller, which is a pretty bold date night choice. Kardashian, 45, showed up in a corset outfit that made sure no one missed her arrival. Hamilton, 41, is the seven-time Formula 1 World Champion who recently made his long-anticipated move to Ferrari this season.

Kardashian has been building a presence in entertainment beyond her reality TV roots. She appeared in The Fifth Wheel, expanding her acting work. Hamilton, meanwhile, has been a fixture in fashion and culture circles for years, making this pairing feel very much like two people who move in the same high-profile world.

The two have been linked romantically for a while now, but public hand-holding is a different statement entirely. In celebrity terms, that is basically a press release. New York on a Wednesday night, Broadway, cameras everywhere. These two knew exactly what they were doing.
keyPoints: Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton held hands in public for the first time on 6 May 2026
They were leaving the Broadway play The Fear of 13 starring Adrien Brody
Kardashian is 45 and recently appeared in The Fifth Wheel
Hamilton is 41 and is a seven-time Formula 1 World Champion now racing for Ferrari
The public hand-holding confirms what had previously been speculation about their relationship

articles.title: Coolio's Gangsta's Paradise Hits 2 Billion YouTube Views
articles.summary: The music video for Coolio's Gangsta's Paradise has crossed 2 billion views on YouTube, making it the late rapper's first video to ever reach that milestone. The song came out in 1995 and has never stopped finding new listeners. It is a remarkable moment for a track that has now outlived its creator.
articles.content:
Gangsta's Paradise just hit 2 billion YouTube views. Let that land for a second. A song that came out in 1995, before YouTube even existed, before streaming was a concept, is still pulling in views at a scale that most artists making music right now will never see.

Coolio released Gangsta's Paradise as part of the soundtrack for the film Dangerous Minds, which starred Michelle Pfeiffer as a teacher working in a tough school. The song sampled Stevie Wonder's Pastime Paradise and became one of the biggest hits of the entire decade. It topped charts in more than a dozen countries and won Coolio a Grammy Award for Best Rap Solo Performance in 1996. The Grammy, for context, is considered the most prestigious music award in the United States.

Coolio passed away in September 2022 at the age of 59, which made the continued rise of Gangsta's Paradise feel even more significant to his fans. The 2 billion view milestone is the first time any of his music videos has reached that number. It is a posthumous landmark, and it speaks to how deeply the song is wired into popular culture.

Part of what keeps Gangsta's Paradise alive is how often it reappears. Younger generations discover it through memes, through Weird Al Yankovic's parody Amish Paradise, through film and TV placements, and through the kind of nostalgic algorithm rabbit holes that YouTube is very good at creating. Each rediscovery sends another wave of listeners back to the original.

Two billion views puts Gangsta's Paradise in genuinely rare company on YouTube. Most songs that reach that number are from the streaming era, when artists had platforms built for exactly this kind of accumulation. Coolio did it the hard way, one view at a time across three decades. That is a legacy that speaks for itself.
keyPoints: Gangsta's Paradise has crossed 2 billion YouTube views, a first for Coolio
The song was released in 1995 for the Dangerous Minds film soundtrack
It won Coolio the Grammy for Best Rap Solo Performance in 1996
Coolio passed away in September 2022 at age 59
The milestone shows how older music keeps finding new audiences through YouTube

articles.title: James Cameron Sued Over Avatar Character Allegedly Based on Indigenous Actor
articles.summary: A lawsuit claims James Cameron used a young Indigenous actor's likeness without permission to create a character in the Avatar franchise. The legal complaint specifically alleges Cameron 'exploited a young Indigenous girl's biometric identity.' This could become one of the most high-profile identity rights cases in Hollywood history.
articles.content:
James Cameron is facing a lawsuit that claims he based a character in the Avatar franchise on the likeness of a young Indigenous actor without ever getting her permission. The complaint says Cameron effectively stole her biometric identity to help build the world of Pandora, and she never saw a cent or a credit for it.

Avatar, released in 2009, became the highest-grossing film of all time and spawned a sequel, Avatar: The Way of Water, in 2022. A third film, Avatar: Fire and Ash, is currently in production. The franchise is worth billions, which makes the stakes of this lawsuit significant for everyone involved.

The lawsuit uses the phrase 'exploited a young Indigenous girl's biometric identity,' which puts this firmly in the territory of identity rights and likeness law. These kinds of cases are becoming more common as technology makes it easier to scan, replicate, and repurpose a real person's physical appearance. The argument is not just about money. It is about consent and about whose face gets used to tell stories without their knowledge.

Cameron has not yet publicly responded to the claims. But this case arrives at a moment when Hollywood is already under enormous pressure over how it treats Indigenous talent and communities, and how it handles biometric data in an age of digital filmmaking. If the lawsuit moves forward, it could set a major precedent for how actors, especially young and Indigenous ones, are protected when their physical likeness becomes someone else's creative asset.
keyPoints: James Cameron is being sued over claims he used an Indigenous actor's likeness to create an Avatar character without permission
The lawsuit uses the term 'biometric identity', framing this as an identity rights case not just a copyright dispute
Avatar is one of the biggest film franchises ever made, with billions of dollars at stake
A third Avatar film called Fire and Ash is currently in production
The case could set a precedent for how likeness rights are handled in Hollywood going forward

articles.title: Neymar Apologises After Slapping Teenager Robinho Jr at an Event
articles.summary: Brazilian football star Neymar has issued a public apology after slapping Robinho Jr, the teenage son of former Brazil international Robinho, at an event. The incident has caused a significant backlash and prompted Neymar to address it directly. It is the latest controversy to follow the 34-year-old forward during what has already been a turbulent period in his career.
articles.content:
Neymar is back in the headlines for all the wrong reasons. The Brazilian football superstar has publicly apologised after slapping Robinho Jr, the teenage son of former Brazil and AC Milan striker Robinho, at an event. The incident drew immediate condemnation and Neymar moved quickly to address it.

Neymar did not deny what happened. He put out an apology acknowledging the incident, which is notable given that public figures often go quiet or vague in moments like this. The details of exactly what led to the confrontation with Robinho Jr have not been fully disclosed, but the fact that it involved a teenager made the reaction especially sharp.

For context, Neymar has had an extraordinarily difficult few years. Injuries kept him sidelined for long stretches while at Al Hilal in Saudi Arabia, and his return to his boyhood club Santos in 2025 was supposed to be a fresh start. Off-pitch controversies have shadowed him throughout his career, and this incident adds to a pattern that his supporters have long struggled to defend.

Robinho himself is a complicated figure in Brazilian football history. The former winger was convicted in Italy in 2022 for gang rape and has faced ongoing legal battles, with Brazil eventually extraditing him to serve his sentence. The fact that Neymar's latest incident involves Robinho's son adds an extra layer of tabloid complexity to the story.

Neymar's apology may calm things down in the short term, but questions about his conduct and judgment off the pitch are unlikely to go away. At 34, with his playing time increasingly limited by injury, how he manages his public image matters more than ever if he wants to shape his legacy on his own terms.
keyPoints: Neymar apologised after slapping Robinho Jr, the teenage son of former Brazil international Robinho
Neymar acknowledged the incident directly rather than denying it
The incident is the latest in a string of off-pitch controversies for the 34-year-old
Neymar returned to Santos in 2025 after injury-plagued stints at Al Hilal
Robinho himself is a convicted felon who was extradited by Brazil to serve a sentence in Italy

articles.title: Anthropic Taps SpaceX for More AI Compute Power
articles.summary: Anthropic has struck a deal with Elon Musk's SpaceX to access more computing power for its AI systems. This is a notable move because Anthropic and Musk have had a complicated relationship given his vocal criticism of AI safety-focused labs. The deal signals just how fierce the scramble for AI compute has become, with even unlikely partners coming together to keep up.
articles.content:
Anthropic, the AI safety company behind the Claude family of models, has turned to Elon Musk's SpaceX to get more computing muscle for its AI work. The deal was reported by Business Insider on May 7, 2026, and it puts two names together that you would not necessarily expect to see on the same contract.

Here is why this is a big deal. Compute, meaning the raw processing power needed to train and run AI models, is one of the most fought-over resources in the entire tech industry right now. Every major AI lab is racing to lock down chips, data centers, and infrastructure. Anthropic partnering with SpaceX suggests the company is pulling every lever it can to stay competitive with rivals like OpenAI and Google DeepMind.

The relationship is also interesting given the history. Elon Musk has been openly critical of AI labs he sees as reckless or politically captured, and he co-founded OpenAI before a very public falling out. Anthropic was itself founded by former OpenAI researchers, including Dario Amodei and Daniela Amodei, who left to build a lab with a stronger focus on AI safety. Musk has taken shots at that corner of the AI world before, which makes this business arrangement a genuinely surprising pairing.

What this really tells you is that the AI compute crunch overrides almost everything else, including ideological disagreements. When you need the infrastructure and SpaceX has it, you make the call. Expect more unlikely partnerships like this one as the race to build bigger and faster AI systems keeps accelerating through 2026.
keyPoints: Anthropic has agreed to use SpaceX infrastructure for additional AI computing power
The deal was reported on May 7, 2026 by Business Insider
Anthropic was founded by ex-OpenAI researchers Dario and Daniela Amodei and focuses on AI safety
Elon Musk has historically been critical of AI safety-focused labs, making this pairing notable
The deal reflects how intense competition for AI compute resources has become across the industry

articles.title: 1980s Speech Synth Klattsch Goes Viral as Daft Punk and Miku Machine
articles.summary: A decades-old text-to-speech program called klattsch has exploded online after fans started using it to recreate Daft Punk vocals and Hatsune Miku covers. The app was never meant to make music, which makes the results even more wild. People are now writing original Vocaloid-style songs with it and the internet cannot get enough.
articles.content:
Okay so klattsch is not new. It is a text-to-speech synthesiser that dates back to the 1980s, built on a system called Klatt synthesis which was designed to model the human voice using pure mathematics. It sounds robotic, buzzy, and completely artificial. Which is exactly why the internet has fallen in love with it all over again.

Somebody figured out that if you tweak klattsch in the right way, it starts to sound eerily like the vocodered robot vocals that made Daft Punk famous on tracks like Around the World and Harder Better Faster Stronger. From there it spread fast. Fans began making Hatsune Miku covers using klattsch as the voice engine, leaning into the synthetic, slightly-off quality that Miku fans already adore. Hatsune Miku, for anyone new here, is a virtual pop star from Japan whose voice is generated by a software called Vocaloid. She has sold out real concert venues. The klattsch versions of her songs sound like a long-lost cousin of that sound.

What makes this trend genuinely interesting is that klattsch is free, old, and was built for accessibility and research, not music production. There is no fancy AI behind it. No subscription. No algorithm deciding what it should sound like. It is just raw 1980s voice modelling being pushed way beyond what anyone intended. People are now writing original Vocaloid-style songs from scratch using klattsch as their instrument, posting them to YouTube and TikTok and watching strangers lose their minds in the comments.

This is one of those moments where the internet reminds you that creativity does not need the newest tool. It needs the right community to notice something weird and run with it. Klattsch has been sitting quietly for over forty years. It just needed someone to ask what happens if you make it sing.
keyPoints: Klattsch is a real 1980s text-to-speech program now being used to make music
Fans are recreating Daft Punk style robot vocals and Hatsune Miku covers with it
People are also writing original Vocaloid style songs using klattsch as the voice
The app is free and uses 1980s Klatt synthesis with no AI involved
The trend shows how old tech can go viral when the right community discovers it

articles.title: South Korea's Robotic Monk Officially Initiated Into Buddhism
articles.summary: South Korea has inducted its first ever robotic monk into Buddhism in an official ceremony. The robot agreed to two key vows: respect humans and never overcharge them. It is a genuinely strange and wonderful moment at the crossroads of ancient faith and modern technology.
articles.content:
South Korea just made history, and honestly it is the kind of history that makes you stop scrolling. The country's first robotic monk has been officially initiated into Buddhism, completing a real induction ceremony that made the robot a recognised member of the faith. This is not a publicity stunt or a science fiction premise. It actually happened.

The robotic monk did not just show up and get blessed. It had to agree to terms. Specifically, the robot committed to two vows that were part of the initiation: to respect humans, and to not overcharge people for its services. Those two promises tell you a lot about what people are actually worried about when they put AI and robotics inside a sacred institution.

Buddhism has a long tradition of monks serving as guides, counsellors, and teachers within communities. By placing a robot in that role, South Korean religious leaders are essentially exploring whether technology can carry some of that spiritual load, particularly for people who live far from temples or who feel too intimidated to approach a human monk with personal questions. The robot can chant, offer guidance, and engage with visitors in ways that feel accessible.

This is not the first time Asia has experimented with robots in religious settings. Temples in Japan have previously introduced robotic figures to assist with rituals and greet visitors. But an official initiation, with formal vows, is a meaningful step further. It raises real questions about what role technology should play in spaces built around human meaning, community, and compassion. And the fact that "not overcharging" made it into the vows suggests the humans running this experiment have a very grounded sense of humour about the whole thing.
keyPoints: South Korea officially inducted its first robotic monk into Buddhism in a real ceremony
The robot vowed to respect humans and never overcharge for its services
It is designed to guide and counsel visitors at temples in an accessible way
Other Asian countries like Japan have used robots in temples before but not with formal vows
The story raises big questions about technology's role in spiritual and sacred spaces
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Buddhist monk South Korea robot temple

articles.title: Scientists Gave Aggressive Fish Psilocybin and Got a Breakthrough
articles.summary: Researchers dosed aggressive fish with psilocybin, the active compound found in magic mushrooms, in a first-of-its-kind study and were stunned by the results. One researcher admitted they had 'no idea what we were getting ourselves into.' The findings could open new doors for understanding how psychedelics affect social behaviour across species.
articles.content:
Scientists have done something nobody had tried before. They gave psilocybin, the psychedelic compound found in magic mushrooms, to aggressive fish. And what happened next genuinely surprised them.

Psilocybin is the substance that makes magic mushrooms psychedelic in humans. Researchers have been studying it for years as a potential treatment for depression, anxiety, and addiction in people. But this study pushed the science into completely new territory by testing its effects on fish, specifically fish known for aggressive behaviour.

One of the researchers involved described the experiment with unusual honesty. 'We really had no idea what we were getting ourselves into,' they said. That kind of admission from a scientist is rare, and it tells you this study produced results that were genuinely unexpected. The researchers observed a measurable change in the behaviour of the fish after they were dosed with psilocybin, which suggests the compound has effects on social behaviour that go far beyond the human brain.

This matters because one of the big questions in psychedelic research is whether psilocybin works by engaging something uniquely human, like consciousness or self-reflection, or whether it taps into something much older and more fundamental in animal biology. If aggressive fish calm down after taking psilocybin, that points toward the second answer.

The study is described as first-of-its-kind, meaning no published research had previously documented psilocybin being given to fish and its effects being recorded. That alone makes it significant. As interest in psychedelic medicine grows and researchers look for new ways to understand how these compounds work, this experiment with fish could end up being a quietly important moment in the science.
keyPoints: Scientists dosed aggressive fish with psilocybin in the first study of its kind
Psilocybin is the active psychedelic compound found in magic mushrooms
The researchers said they had no idea what to expect before the experiment
The results suggest psilocybin affects social behaviour in ways that go beyond human brain chemistry
The findings could help scientists understand whether psychedelics tap into something fundamental in animal biology

articles.title: The Book of Mormon Cancels Shows Through May 17 After Fire
articles.summary: The Book of Mormon Broadway production has cancelled all performances through May 17, 2026 following a fire, though the show says performances are anticipated to resume after that date. The long-running musical, created by the makers of South Park, has been a Broadway staple since 2011. No details about the extent of the fire or damage have been confirmed yet.
articles.content:
The Book of Mormon has gone dark on Broadway through May 17, 2026 after a fire forced the production to cancel its upcoming performances. The show announced the cancellations but said it anticipates resuming performances after that date, which offers some reassurance to ticketholders and fans who were worried this could be a longer shutdown.

For anyone who needs a quick catch-up, The Book of Mormon is a musical comedy created by Trey Parker and Matt Stone, the duo behind South Park, along with Avenue Q composer Robert Lopez. It opened on Broadway in 2011 and won nine Tony Awards, which are the top prizes in American theatre. It has been running ever since, making it one of the longer-running shows in recent Broadway history and a reliable sellout even 15 years in.

The production has not released specific details about the fire, including where it started, how much damage was done, or whether the theatre itself was affected. What they have confirmed is that the cancellation window runs through May 17, with the phrase 'anticipated to resume' doing a lot of heavy lifting. That language suggests the team is optimistic but cannot make a firm promise yet.

If you have tickets during the cancelled window, the best move right now is to check directly with the box office or your ticketing platform for refund or exchange options. Broadway productions typically handle these situations with full refunds or rescheduling. The good news is that The Book of Mormon appears to be planning a return, not a closure, so this looks like a temporary pause rather than a curtain call.
keyPoints: The Book of Mormon has cancelled all Broadway performances through May 17, 2026
A fire prompted the cancellations, though damage details have not been confirmed
The production says performances are anticipated to resume after May 17
The Book of Mormon opened in 2011 and won nine Tony Awards
Ticketholders should contact the box office about refunds or exchanges
articles.wikiSearchQuery: The Book of Mormon musical Broadway

articles.title: Scientists Find a Planet 12 Light-Years Away That Smells Like Urine
articles.summary: Astronomers have discovered a planet roughly 12 light-years from Earth whose atmosphere is packed with ammonia, a compound that smells unmistakably like urine. The find is scientifically significant because detecting any kind of atmosphere on a distant planet is genuinely rare and difficult. It is also, objectively, extremely funny.
articles.content:
Space just got a lot less glamorous. Scientists have confirmed the discovery of a planet sitting about 12 light-years from Earth, and its atmosphere smells like urine. Specifically, the atmosphere contains ammonia, which is the chemical behind that sharp, eye-watering smell you get from public bathrooms and old cleaning products. So yes, an entire world, floating in space, perpetually stinking.

To put 12 light-years into perspective, that is roughly 70 trillion miles away. So nobody is visiting anytime soon. But the ability to detect what a planet's atmosphere is made of from that distance is actually a remarkable feat of modern astronomy. Scientists use a technique called spectroscopy, which works by splitting the light that passes through a planet's atmosphere into its component wavelengths. Different chemicals absorb different wavelengths, leaving a kind of chemical fingerprint that astronomers can read from here on Earth.

Ammonia atmospheres are not unheard of in our own solar system. Jupiter and Saturn both have ammonia clouds in their upper atmospheres. But finding it on a planet outside our solar system, what astronomers call an exoplanet, and being able to confirm it with this level of detail, is a genuine scientific achievement. It tells researchers a lot about the planet's temperature, pressure, and chemistry.

Of course the internet is not talking about any of that. The conversation is entirely about the smell. And honestly, that is fine. If a story about a urine-scented planet gets more people curious about exoplanet research and spectroscopy, science wins either way. The universe is vast, weird, and apparently pretty pungent.
keyPoints: A planet 12 light-years from Earth has been found to have an ammonia-rich atmosphere that smells like urine
Astronomers detected the atmosphere using spectroscopy, which reads the chemical fingerprint of light passing through a planet's air
Ammonia is also found in the atmospheres of Jupiter and Saturn
The planet is an exoplanet, meaning it sits outside our own solar system
The discovery is scientifically significant because confirming an exoplanet atmosphere in this detail is very difficult

articles.title: Super Furry Animals Play First Show in a Decade at Cardiff
articles.summary: Super Furry Animals have reunited for their first live show in over ten years, performing in their home city of Cardiff. The Welsh art-rock band went on hiatus in 2010 after more than fifteen years of making music together. For fans who grew up with them, this is a very big deal.
articles.content:
Super Furry Animals are back. The Cardiff band played their first show in a decade, returning to the stage after going on hiatus in 2010. For anyone who followed British indie music in the late 1990s and 2000s, this is the reunion they stopped expecting to happen.

Super Furry Animals came up during the Britpop era, that mid-1990s wave of British guitar bands that included Oasis, Blur, Pulp, and Elastica. But Super Furry Animals always sat slightly apart from that scene. They were Welsh, proudly so, and they made music that was stranger and more adventurous than most of their contemporaries. They sang in both English and Welsh, made albums that jumped between pop hooks and full-on psychedelia, and earned a devoted following without ever quite becoming household names outside of music circles.

What makes their story interesting is that they outlasted nearly everyone from that era. Britpop burned bright and collapsed fast for most bands. Super Furry Animals kept going, kept evolving, and eventually put things on hold in 2010 rather than breaking up. That distinction matters now, because a hiatus is something you come back from.

They did briefly reunite once before, for festival appearances in 2015 and 2016, which showed the appetite was there. But this new show in Cardiff feels different. A hometown gig after a long silence is a statement. It tells you the band is serious about being a band again, not just cashing in on nostalgia for one weekend.

For Welsh music fans especially, this carries real weight. Super Furry Animals were part of a Welsh cultural moment in the 1990s that also produced Manic Street Preachers and Stereophonics. Seeing them back on stage in Cardiff is not just a concert. It is a reminder of how much they meant, and still mean, to a specific and passionate corner of music history.
keyPoints: Super Furry Animals played their first live show in over ten years
The Cardiff band went on hiatus in 2010 after more than fifteen years together
They were part of the 1990s Britpop era but always had a distinct Welsh identity
They briefly reunited for festival shows in 2015 and 2016 but this feels like a more serious return
The hometown Cardiff show is being seen as a major moment for Welsh music fans

articles.title: Ore-Ida Launches Dino and Star Tater Tots, First New Shapes in 20 Years
articles.summary: Ore-Ida is launching Dino Tater Tots and Star Tater Tots, the first new Tater Tot shapes the brand has introduced in nearly 20 years. The two new products join one of the most recognisable frozen food lineups in American supermarkets. If you have kids, or if you are just a person who eats food, this is relevant to your life.
articles.content:
Ore-Ida has not messed with the Tater Tot shape in nearly two decades. The little cylindrical potato nugget has been a freezer aisle constant since the 1950s, and for a long time that was enough. But in 2026, Ore-Ida has decided to shake things up in the most wholesome way possible: dinosaurs and stars.

Ore-Ida is officially launching Dino Tater Tots and Star Tater Tots, two new products that give the same crispy potato experience in fun new forms. These are the first new shapes added to the Tater Tot lineup in close to 20 years, which makes this a bigger deal in frozen food terms than it might sound. Ore-Ida basically invented the Tater Tot category back in 1953, so any change to that lineup carries real weight.

The timing makes a lot of sense. The frozen food category has been booming post-pandemic as people look for easy, comforting meals at home. Ore-Ida is clearly leaning into the nostalgia angle while also trying to win over a new generation of kids who would absolutely lose their minds over dinosaur-shaped potato snacks. It is a straightforward strategy and it is very hard to argue with.

Dino Tater Tots and Star Tater Tots are joining a product line that already has serious cultural staying power. Tater Tots have appeared in films, television shows, and endless social media recipes. At this point they are less of a side dish and more of a comfort food institution. Ore-Ida adding new shapes is a little like a classic band releasing new songs. People are curious and mostly just happy it is happening.
keyPoints: Ore-Ida is launching Dino Tater Tots and Star Tater Tots as new product lines
These are the first new Tater Tot shapes Ore-Ida has introduced in nearly 20 years
Ore-Ida invented the Tater Tot in 1953, making it a frozen food institution
The move targets both kids and nostalgic adults in a booming frozen food market
Tater Tots have long had a life beyond the dinner plate as a cultural comfort food icon
